architectReview.evidence: Desktop-first architect review approved the shipped scope with no blockers. Evidence: .gjc/_session-019f15b3-9ce4-7000-81c8-6a3a2b4443a0/plans/ralplan/019f15b3-9ce4-7000-81c8-6a3a2b4443a0/stage-01-architect.md states the current shipped scope is aligned with the narrowed desktop-first launch contract and explicitly recommends approval.

executorQa.evidence: Desktop-first verification passed with npm test, npm run build, and npm run qa:matrix. qa:matrix verified desktop Chromium and desktop Firefox, captured parity for text/haze/noisy 12MP fixtures, and exercised both optional and forced downscale oversize paths. Desktop smoke evidence and updated adversarial review artifacts were recorded.

artifactRefs.description: Desktop Chromium and desktop Firefox automation transcript with timings, parity metrics, downloads, and oversize flows.
Desktop-first browser automation summary distilled from the shipped QA matrix and smoke evidence.
Non-uniform full-page screenshot of the ready state with visible image comparison content.
Desktop-first adversarial review covering support scope, parity tolerance, and optional plus forced oversize handling.

contractCoverage.contractRef: Desktop-first support matrix in pending-approval.md and README.md
contractCoverage.obligation: Desktop Chromium and desktop Firefox must complete single-image upload, enhancement, compare, and PNG/JPG export within the approved desktop scope.

contractCoverage.contractRef: Desktop-first launch boundary in pending-approval.md, README.md, and src/lib/capabilities.ts
contractCoverage.obligation: Mobile browsers are outside the official V1 launch scope.

surfaceEvidence.invocation: Automated desktop matrix run via npm run qa:matrix
Capability gating and documentation review plus desktop smoke evidence

adversarialCases.scenario: 16.6MP optional original/downscale choice and 28MP forced downscale guardrail on the supported desktop matrix.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The app must require an explicit decision for oversized images and disable original-size processing when the guardrail is exceeded.

adversarialCases.scenario: Treat mobile browsers as out of scope while preserving the supported desktop launch surface.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The shipped contract, UI messaging, and QA gate must keep mobile outside official support without weakening desktop verification.

iteration.evidence: After narrowing the launch claim to desktop-first support, the verification loop was rerun cleanly: npm test passed, npm run build passed, and npm run qa:matrix passed with desktop-only targets and refreshed artifacts.
